name: Object-Oriented Programming
def: Organises software as objects bundling state with behaviour, communicating via messages/methods.
problem: Model domains as interacting entities while encapsulating state and enabling polymorphic substitution.
mechanism: Encapsulation hides state behind methods; inheritance and interfaces enable subtype polymorphism and dynamic dispatch; composition assembles behaviour.
benefits: Maps well to domain entities and GUIs
limits: Can obscure data layout / hurt performance
failures: God objects and fragile base classes
Anemic models where logic leaks out
myths: "OOP = inheritance" — composition and interfaces matter more than class trees
